papers.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector
papers.brief_description: Classically conformal (Coleman-Weinberg) hidden U(1)' dark sector with a complex dark scalar that radiatively breaks the symmetry, a dark photon, and a stable Dirac dark fermion that gets its mass from a Yukawa coupling to the dark scalar. A supercooled FOPT in the MeV range gives the NANOGrav nHz GW signal while the fermion is sub-GeV dark matter. This is essentially the same model as the target, with an added kinetic-mixing portal to the SM.
papers.model_studied: Classically conformal dark U(1)' with complex scalar (radial = dilaton, angular eaten), dark photon A' (m_A' = g*v), Dirac dark fermion chi with Yukawa y (m_chi = y*v/sqrt2), CW radiative breaking. Kinetic mixing to SM hypercharge for phenomenology.
papers.similarity_to_target: Near-identical structural match: conformal U(1)_D, CW SSB, complex scalar, Z'/dark photon, Dirac dark fermion via Yukawa, supercooled FOPT, NANOGrav nHz target. Differences: target adds an explicit mirror fermion for anomaly cancellation and neglects kinetic mixing for the FOPT; this paper uses kinetic mixing as a portal.
assumptions: CW radiative breaking at one loop

papers.title: Supercooled phase transitions in conformal dark sectors explain NANOGrav data
papers.brief_description: Conformal dark U(1)' model with radiative (Coleman-Weinberg) symmetry breaking producing a massive Z' and a light scalon. Supercooled FOPT explains NANOGrav nHz data; argues such transitions CAN fit PTA contrary to some prior claims. Provides a best-fit benchmark.
papers.model_studied: Dark U(1)' with conformal scalar potential, scalon (radial pseudo-dilaton), massive Z', small kinetic mixing to SM. CW radiative SSB.
papers.similarity_to_target: Strong match for the scalar+gauge sector and CW/conformal mechanism and PTA target. Does not emphasize the Dirac dark fermion/Yukawa sector that the target includes.

papers.title: Supercooled Dark Scalar Phase Transitions explanation of NANOGrav data
papers.brief_description: Minimal dark U(1) gauge group plus a dark scalar undergoing a strongly supercooled FOPT in the 100 MeV - GeV range to explain the NANOGrav nHz SGWB. Tracks the transition from onset to completion including vacuum domination and finds a correlation between gauge coupling and quartic. Provides four benchmark points.
papers.model_studied: Dark U(1) with complex/dark scalar, massive Z' (m_Zp = g_D*v), light dark scalar m_phi. Supercooled FOPT; close to conformal regime (small quartic) with g_D-lambda correlation.
papers.similarity_to_target: Strong match in scalar+gauge sector, PTA target, supercooled regime, vev/scale in 0.5-10 GeV. Treats lambda as a small free parameter rather than strictly CW-fixed; no dedicated dark-fermion DM Yukawa sector emphasized.

papers.title: Gravitational waves from a dark U(1)_D phase transition in the light of NANOGrav 12.5 yr data
papers.brief_description: Early study of a strong sub-electroweak FOPT in a U(1)_D extension with a complex singlet scalar and U(1)_D vector boson, fitting NANOGrav 12.5yr. A U(1)_D-charged singlet fermion can be (non-thermal) dark matter. Establishes the minimal dark U(1)_D + GW + NANOGrav program.
papers.model_studied: U(1)_D extension with complex singlet scalar and Z'; thermal effective potential FOPT (not strictly classically scale-invariant; uses standard finite-T potential, generally with explicit/soft mass terms).
papers.similarity_to_target: Same dark U(1)_D + complex scalar + Z' + optional charged dark fermion and same NANOGrav target, but pre-15yr data and not in the classically scale-invariant / Coleman-Weinberg regime, so its benchmark numbers are background/qualitative rather than directly transferable.

papers.title: NANOGrav Signal from a Dark Conformal Phase Transition
papers.brief_description: A nearly-conformal dark sector (dark Yang-Mills / dilaton-radion, holographic dual) with a confining/dilaton phase transition explaining NANOGrav, avoiding DeltaN_eff constraints via a decoupled dark thermal history. Conformal-PT-for-NANOGrav context but a different (non-Abelian confinement/dilaton) mechanism.
papers.model_studied: Nearly conformal dark Yang-Mills with light dilaton/radion, dark glueballs; confinement/dilaton FOPT.
papers.similarity_to_target: Shares the conformal-PT + NANOGrav theme and supercooling/dilaton physics, but the symmetry-breaking mechanism (strong dynamics / radion) differs from the target's weakly-coupled CW gauged U(1). Useful for context and DeltaN_eff/thermal-decoupling discussion only.
assumptions: nearly conformal strong dynamics / 5D holographic dual

novelty_notes: The target model (classically scale-invariant / Coleman-Weinberg gauged dark U(1)_D with complex scalar Phi, dark photon Z', and a Dirac dark fermion chi obtaining mass via a Yukawa to Phi, driving a strongly supercooled FOPT for the NANOGrav nHz signal) is very closely matched in the literature, most directly by Balan, Bringmann, Kahlhoefer, Matuszak & Tasillo (arXiv:2502.19478, JCAP 08(2025)062), which is essentially the same model including the Yukawa-mass dark fermion as sub-GeV DM. Closely related conformal/supercooled dark U(1) PTA explanations are Goncalves, Marfatia, Morais & Pasechnik (2501.11619) and Costa et al. (2501.15649). The mostly-novel ingredient of the target is the explicit mirror fermion (eta) introduced purely for U(1)_D anomaly cancellation with the chiral chi charges and the deliberate neglect of kinetic mixing for the FOPT; the FOPT phenomenology itself is well-precedented.

conventions.note: same vev and mass conventions as target handoff

benchmark_hints.notes: Coupled benchmark (Point A): moderate supercooling alpha~5, beta/H~21. Maps directly onto target free parameters (g_D, y, w=v). Best single anchor point. Point B (g_D=0.79, y=0.401, v=343 MeV, alpha=62.6, beta/H=13.3) gives stronger supercooling.

conventions.potential: small-quartic near-conformal (lambda treated free, not strictly CW-fixed)

benchmark_hints.notes: Spans w = 0.5-10 GeV, overlapping the target's GeV-scale window. Very strong supercooling (alpha~10^2-10^3). Convert H*R* to beta/H downstream. lambda is an input here vs CW-dependent in target.

Genuine CW/conformal best fit with very strong supercooling (alpha~10^5). m_Zp/v consistent with g_D~0.59. No explicit Yukawa fermion sector. Useful to bracket the strong-supercooling end of g_D-space.

theory_uncertainties: Renormalization-scheme / scale choice in the CW potential (target fixes mu = w, no RG running, no Daisy; literature varies and 2511.02910 cautions on Daisy vs 3D-EFT treatment of supercooled CW PTs).
Percolation vs nucleation vs reheating temperature definitions differ across papers (T_p at 34% true vacuum vs T_*; reheating after vacuum domination). Match the backend's definitions carefully.
GW strength measure not uniform: alpha+beta/H (2502.19478, 2501.11619) vs alpha+H*R* (2501.15649). Convert consistently for the spectrum template.
Whether lambda is strictly CW-fixed (target, 2502.19478, 2501.11619) or a small free input (2501.15649) shifts the g_D-lambda relation and the supercooling depth.
recommended_next_steps: Adopt arXiv:2502.19478 Point A (g_D=0.877, y=0.363, w=92.9 MeV) as the primary anchor benchmark; it shares the target's exact field content and mass conventions.
Use arXiv:2501.15649 BP1-BP4 (g_D~0.75-0.86, w=0.5-10 GeV) to scan the GeV-scale vev window of the target and bracket strong supercooling.
Cross-check g_D-only conformal limit (small y) against arXiv:2501.11619 (g_D=0.59, alpha~1e5) for the deep-supercooling regime.
Reconcile the target backend's T_*/T_p/T_reh and alpha vs beta/H vs H*R* definitions with each cited paper before transferring numbers.
Flag for critic-agent: the model is structurally non-novel (closest = 2502.19478); the only distinctive feature is the anomaly-canceling mirror fermion eta and neglected kinetic mixing.
